items.headline: City launching amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers and small businesses, likely allowing operators to become compliant without penalties. This could affect how many informal or under-the-radar businesses operating in the city come into the open.

items.headline: New incentives coming for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council is considering economic development incentives to attract or support retail shops and startups. This signals an effort to strengthen Cupertino's local business ecosystem beyond just tech companies.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road through a development agreement. This project will add housing to an area near major employment centers.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application to California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) to secure state funding for housing development. If awarded, this could accelerate affordable housing projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeks public input on $2026-27 housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is asking residents to comment on how it will spend federal HUD housing grant funds for the coming fiscal year. This is your chance to influence priorities like affordable housing, homelessness services, or neighborhood improvements.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking program. This appears to be a new or expanded initiative to address vehicle dwelling and homelessness in the city.
